prophecies.a:
无力回天，鞠躬尽瘁。阴居阳拂，八千女鬼。

白话文翻译：无力挽回天命，鞠躬尽瘁而死。阴气占据而阳气消退，「八千女鬼」合为「魏」字。
prophecies.a_en:
Powerless to reverse heaven's will, he bows and exhausts himself to the end. Yin occupies while yang recedes; 'eight thousand female ghosts' form the character 'Wei.'

Vernacular: Unable to reverse the mandate of heaven, he serves with utmost devotion until death. Yin prevails while yang fades — 'eight thousand female ghosts' combine to form the character 'Wei' (魏), referring to the state of Wei.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「鞠躬尽瘁」正是诸葛亮自身写照，出自其《出师表》。蜀汉于263年被魏所灭。「八千女鬼」拆字合为「魏」字（「鬼」+「委」中含「女」和「禾」八划），精确预言蜀汉亡于曹魏。
prophecies.verdict_en: 'Bowing and exhausting himself' perfectly describes Zhuge Liang himself, echoing his own Memorial on Dispatching the Troops. Shu Han was conquered by Wei in 263 AD. 'Eight thousand female ghosts' is a character puzzle that forms 'Wei' (魏), accurately predicting Shu Han's fall to Cao Wei.
prophecies.yearReason: 根据马前课卦辞的传统解读对应该历史时期
prophecies.yearReason_en: Traditional interpretation of Maqianke divination corresponds to this historical period

prophecies.a:
火上有火，光烛中土。称名不正，江东有虎。

白话文翻译：火上加火为「炎」，光照中原大地。名号不正统，江东有虎踞。
prophecies.a_en:
Fire upon fire, its light illuminates the Central Land. The title claimed is not rightful; in Jiangdong there is a tiger.

Vernacular: Fire upon fire forms 'Yan' (炎, as in Sima Yan); its light shines across the Central Plain. The claim to the throne is not legitimate; east of the river, a tiger lurks.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「火上有火」合为「炎」字，指司马炎（晋武帝），265年篡魏建立西晋，280年灭吴统一天下。「称名不正」指司马氏以禅让之名行篡位之实。「江东有虎」指东吴孙氏政权盘踞江东。
prophecies.verdict_en: 'Fire upon fire' forms the character 'Yan' (炎), referring to Sima Yan (Emperor Wu of Jin), who usurped Wei in 265 AD and established the Western Jin, unifying China by conquering Wu in 280. 'Title not rightful' refers to the Sima clan seizing power through forced abdication. 'Tiger in Jiangdong' refers to the Sun family's Wu kingdom in the east.
prophecies.yearReason: 根据马前课卦辞的传统解读对应该历史时期
prophecies.yearReason_en: Traditional interpretation of Maqianke divination corresponds to this historical period

prophecies.a:
扰扰中原，山河无主。二三其位，羊终马始。

白话文翻译：中原纷乱不休，山河没有真正的主人。帝位频繁更迭，从羊（晋）结束到马（司马）开始。
prophecies.a_en:
Turmoil across the Central Plain, mountains and rivers without a master. The throne changes hands again and again; it ends with the sheep and begins with the horse.

Vernacular: The Central Plain is in endless turmoil, its lands without a true ruler. The imperial throne changes hands repeatedly — ending with 'sheep' and beginning with 'horse.'
prophecies.verdict_zh: 预言南北朝时期（420-589）中原大乱，政权频繁更替。「二三其位」暗指南朝宋齐梁陈、北朝多个政权交替。「羊终马始」一说指司马氏（马）的晋朝终结后进入乱世，也有解为此课对应的干支纪年。南北朝历时近170年，战乱频仍，与预言完全吻合。
prophecies.verdict_en: Predicts the chaos of the Northern and Southern Dynasties period (420-589), with frequent regime changes across the Central Plain. 'Throne changes again and again' alludes to the succession of Southern dynasties (Song, Qi, Liang, Chen) and multiple Northern regimes. 'Ending with sheep, beginning with horse' is interpreted as referring to the Sima (horse) clan's Jin dynasty ending and the era of division beginning. The period lasted nearly 170 years of constant warfare, matching the prophecy.
prophecies.yearReason: 根据马前课卦辞的传统解读对应该历史时期
prophecies.yearReason_en: Traditional interpretation of Maqianke divination corresponds to this historical period

prophecies.a:
十八男儿，起于太原。动则得解，日月丽天。

白话文翻译：「十八」与「男儿」的「子」合为「李」字，从太原起兵。行动即可解困，日月丽天象征大唐盛世。
prophecies.a_en:
Eighteen boys arise from Taiyuan. Move and you shall find release; sun and moon shine together in the sky.

Vernacular: 'Eighteen' and 'boy' combine to form the character 'Li' (李); they rise from Taiyuan. Action brings liberation; sun and moon adorning the sky symbolize the glory of the Tang.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「十八男儿」拆字：「十」+「八」+「子」=「李」，指李渊从太原起兵建立唐朝（618年）。「动则得解」暗含「易」字（解卦），也暗示世民之「民」。「日月丽天」的「日月」合为「明」，但此处指唐朝如日月般光辉灿烂。唐朝（618-907）是中国历史上最强盛的朝代之一。
prophecies.verdict_en: 'Eighteen boys': '十' + '八' + '子' = '李' (Li), referring to Li Yuan who rose from Taiyuan to found the Tang dynasty in 618 AD. 'Move and find release' contains a hidden reference to 'Yi' (easy/change). 'Sun and moon in the sky' signifies the Tang dynasty's brilliance. The Tang dynasty (618-907) was one of the most powerful dynasties in Chinese history.
prophecies.yearReason: 根据马前课卦辞的传统解读对应该历史时期
prophecies.yearReason_en: Traditional interpretation of Maqianke divination corresponds to this historical period

prophecies.a:
五十年中，其数有八。小人道长，生灵荼毒。

白话文翻译：五十年间，其中有「八」之数。小人当道日渐猖獗，生灵遭受荼毒。
prophecies.a_en:
In fifty years, the number is eight. The way of petty men grows long; living souls are poisoned and tormented.

Vernacular: Within fifty years, the number eight appears. Petty men's influence grows; the people suffer greatly.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 预言五代十国时期（907-960）。「五十年中」指五代历时约53年。「其数有八」一说指五代中有八姓十三君，也有解为后世所记的纷乱局势。「小人道长，生灵荼毒」准确描述了五代时期军阀混战、民不聊生的惨状。
prophecies.verdict_en: Predicts the Five Dynasties and Ten Kingdoms period (907-960). 'Fifty years' refers to the approximately 53-year span. 'The number eight' is interpreted as referring to the eight surnames among thirteen rulers of the Five Dynasties. 'Petty men's way grows long, living souls tormented' accurately describes the warlord chaos and widespread suffering of this era.
prophecies.yearReason: 根据马前课卦辞的传统解读对应该历史时期
prophecies.yearReason_en: Traditional interpretation of Maqianke divination corresponds to this historical period

prophecies.a:
惟天生水，顺天应人。刚中柔外，土乃生金。

白话文翻译：天一生水，顺应天命合乎民心。外柔内刚，土（宋）生金（金朝）。
prophecies.a_en:
Heaven alone gives birth to water; obey heaven and respond to the people. Hard within, soft without; earth gives birth to metal.

Vernacular: Heaven gives birth to water — obey the mandate of heaven and respond to the people's will. Firm inside yet yielding outside; earth (Song) gives birth to metal (Jin).
prophecies.verdict_zh: 预言宋朝（960-1279）。「惟天生水」暗指宋朝五行属火（赵匡胤以「宋」为国号），但「天生水」也可解为天命所归。「顺天应人」指赵匡胤陈桥兵变、黄袍加身，以和平方式取得政权。「刚中柔外」精确概括宋朝重文轻武的国策。「土乃生金」暗示金朝崛起，北宋亡于金。
prophecies.verdict_en: Predicts the Song dynasty (960-1279). 'Obey heaven, respond to the people' refers to Zhao Kuangyin's relatively peaceful seizure of power through the Chenqiao mutiny. 'Hard within, soft without' precisely captures the Song's policy of emphasizing civil governance over military strength. 'Earth gives birth to metal' foreshadows the rise of the Jin dynasty, which conquered Northern Song.
prophecies.yearReason: 根据马前课卦辞的传统解读对应该历史时期
prophecies.yearReason_en: Traditional interpretation of Maqianke divination corresponds to this historical period

prophecies.a:
一元复始，以刚处中。五五相传，尔西我东。

白话文翻译：一个新的纪元重新开始，以刚强居于天下之中。五五相传（暗指元朝传承），你在西边我在东边（东西分裂）。
prophecies.a_en:
One cycle begins anew, ruling the center with firmness. Five and five are passed down; you in the west, I in the east.

Vernacular: A new era begins, holding the center with strength. Five-five transmission (alluding to Yuan's succession); you in the west, I in the east (east-west division).
prophecies.verdict_zh: 预言元朝（1271-1368）。「一元复始」直接点出「元」字，指忽必烈建立元朝。「以刚处中」描述蒙古族以武力征服中原。「五五相传」指元朝传了约十位皇帝（一说指元朝国祚不足百年）。「尔西我东」指蒙古帝国分裂为多个汗国，东西各据。
prophecies.verdict_en: Predicts the Yuan dynasty (1271-1368). 'One cycle begins anew' directly contains 'Yuan' (元, meaning 'origin/beginning'), referring to Kublai Khan founding the Yuan dynasty. 'Ruling the center with firmness' describes the Mongol conquest of China by force. 'Five-five passed down' refers to approximately ten Yuan emperors. 'You west, I east' refers to the fragmentation of the Mongol Empire into separate khanates.
prophecies.yearReason: 根据马前课卦辞的传统解读对应该历史时期
prophecies.yearReason_en: Traditional interpretation of Maqianke divination corresponds to this historical period

prophecies.a:
日月丽天，其色若赤。绵绵延延，凡十六叶。

白话文翻译：日月丽天合为「明」字，其色红赤。国祚绵延，共传十六代。
prophecies.a_en:
Sun and moon adorn the sky, their color like crimson. Continuous and unbroken, sixteen generations in all.

Vernacular: Sun and moon in the sky form 'Ming' (明, 'bright'); the color is crimson-red. The dynasty extends continuously for sixteen generations.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「日月丽天」的「日月」合为「明」字，直接预言明朝（1368-1644）。「其色若赤」指朱（红色）姓，即明太祖朱元璋。「绵绵延延，凡十六叶」精确预言明朝传了十六位皇帝（从太祖到崇祯），数目完全吻合。
prophecies.verdict_en: 'Sun and moon in the sky' combines to form 'Ming' (明), directly predicting the Ming dynasty (1368-1644). 'Color like crimson' refers to the surname Zhu (朱, meaning 'vermilion/red'), i.e., Ming founder Zhu Yuanzhang. 'Sixteen generations' precisely predicts the Ming dynasty's sixteen emperors (from Taizu to Chongzhen) — an exact match.
prophecies.yearReason: 根据马前课卦辞的传统解读对应该历史时期
prophecies.yearReason_en: Traditional interpretation of Maqianke divination corresponds to this historical period

prophecies.a:
水月有主，古月为君。十传绝统，相敬若宾。

白话文翻译：「水月有主」暗含「清」字，「古月」合为「胡」字指满族。传了十代之后统绪断绝，君臣相敬如宾。
prophecies.a_en:
Water and moon have a master; ancient moon becomes the ruler. After ten transmissions the lineage ends; they treat each other with the courtesy of guests.

Vernacular: 'Water and moon have a master' alludes to the character 'Qing' (清); 'ancient moon' forms 'Hu' (胡, meaning foreign/barbarian). After ten reigns the succession ends; ruler and subject treat each other as honored guests.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「水月有主」暗含「清」字（三点水+主+月的组合暗示）。「古月为君」合为「胡」，指满族入主中原。「十传绝统」指清朝传了十帝后覆灭（从顺治到宣统共十帝）。「相敬若宾」暗示清末推行立宪、优待皇室的和平过渡。清朝（1644-1912）的兴亡与预言高度吻合。
prophecies.verdict_en: 'Water and moon have a master' alludes to 'Qing' (清). 'Ancient moon becomes ruler' forms 'Hu' (胡, foreigner), referring to the Manchu conquest. 'Ten transmissions, lineage ends' — the Qing had ten emperors from Shunzhi to Xuantong. 'Courtesy of guests' suggests the relatively peaceful transition at the dynasty's end, with the abdication agreement and favorable treatment of the imperial family. The Qing dynasty (1644-1912) matches closely.
prophecies.yearReason: 根据马前课卦辞的传统解读对应该历史时期
prophecies.yearReason_en: Traditional interpretation of Maqianke divination corresponds to this historical period

prophecies.a:
豕后牛前，千人一口。五二倒置，朋来无咎。

白话文翻译：猪年之后牛年之前（即鼠年，1912年为壬子鼠年），「千人一口」合为「和」字。五二倒置（二五为十，暗指中华民国），朋友到来没有灾咎。
prophecies.a_en:
After the pig, before the ox; a thousand people, one mouth. Five-two inverted; friends come without blame.

Vernacular: After the year of the pig, before the year of the ox (i.e., the year of the rat — 1912 was a Renzi/rat year). 'A thousand people, one mouth' forms the character 'He' (和, harmony). Five-two inverted; friends arrive without misfortune.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「豕后牛前」即鼠年，1912年（壬子年）清帝退位、中华民国成立。「千人一口」合为「和」字，暗指共和体制。「五二倒置」有多种解读：一说二五为十，指中华民国（「十」字形似「中」字简写）；一说暗指民国政局反复。「朋来无咎」指国际社会承认民国。辛亥革命推翻帝制、建立共和，与预言吻合。
prophecies.verdict_en: 'After pig, before ox' = rat year. 1912 (Renzi, year of the rat) saw the Qing emperor's abdication and the founding of the Republic of China. 'Thousand people, one mouth' forms 'He' (和, harmony), alluding to the republican system. 'Five-two inverted' has multiple interpretations, possibly referring to the Republic. 'Friends come without blame' suggests international recognition. The Xinhai Revolution overthrowing the imperial system matches the prophecy.
prophecies.yearReason: 根据马前课卦辞的传统解读对应该历史时期
prophecies.yearReason_en: Traditional interpretation of Maqianke divination corresponds to this historical period

prophecies.a:
四门乍辟，突如其来。晨鸡一声，其道大衰。

白话文翻译：四方门户突然打开，变故突如其来。晨鸡一声啼鸣（鸡年），其道运开始大衰。
prophecies.a_en:
Four gates suddenly open; it comes without warning. The morning rooster crows once; the way begins its great decline.

Vernacular: The four gates suddenly swing open; change arrives without warning. The morning rooster crows once (the year of the rooster); the way enters its great decline.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「四门乍辟」一般解读为抗日战争时期中国国门被打开。「突如其来」指日本侵华的突然袭击。「晨鸡一声」指1945年（乙酉鸡年）日本投降。「其道大衰」指此后国民党政权迅速衰败，在内战中败退台湾。1937年日本全面侵华到1949年中华人民共和国成立，与预言的历史脉络基本吻合。
prophecies.verdict_en: 'Four gates open' is generally interpreted as China being forced open during the Japanese invasion. 'Comes without warning' refers to Japan's sudden attacks. 'Morning rooster crows' refers to 1945 (Year of the Rooster, Yiyou) when Japan surrendered. 'The way greatly declines' refers to the rapid decline of the Nationalist government, which retreated to Taiwan after losing the civil war. The period from 1937 (full-scale Japanese invasion) to 1949 (founding of the PRC) broadly matches.
prophecies.yearReason: 根据马前课卦辞的传统解读对应该历史时期
prophecies.yearReason_en: Traditional interpretation of Maqianke divination corresponds to this historical period

prophecies.a:
拯患救难，是唯圣人。阳复而治，晦极生明。

白话文翻译：拯救患难，唯有圣人能做到。阳气恢复天下大治，黑暗到极点便会生出光明。
prophecies.a_en:
To rescue from peril and save from calamity — only the sage can do this. When yang returns, order is restored; when darkness reaches its extreme, light is born.

Vernacular: Rescuing the world from peril and calamity — this is the work of the sage alone. When yang energy returns, governance is restored; when darkness reaches its nadir, brightness is born.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 一般解读为预言中华人民共和国成立（1949年）。「拯患救难，是唯圣人」被解读为指开国领袖。「阳复而治」指新政权建立后恢复秩序。「晦极生明」暗示经历长期战乱后迎来和平。但此课解读存在较大争议，不同版本和注释者的理解差异很大，部分学者认为此课可能指更长的历史时期而非某一具体事件。
prophecies.verdict_en: Generally interpreted as predicting the founding of the People's Republic of China (1949). 'Only the sage can rescue' is read as referring to the founding leader. 'Yang returns, order restored' refers to the new regime restoring stability. 'Darkness extreme, light born' suggests peace after prolonged warfare. However, this lesson's interpretation is highly disputed — different editions and commentators vary significantly, and some scholars argue it may refer to a broader historical period rather than one specific event.
prophecies.yearReason: 根据马前课卦辞的传统解读对应该历史时期
prophecies.yearReason_en: Traditional interpretation of Maqianke divination corresponds to this historical period

prophecies.a:
贤不遗野，天下一家。无名无德，光耀中华。

白话文翻译：贤才不被遗落在民间，天下如同一家。没有个人名望和功德的标榜，光辉照耀中华大地。
prophecies.a_en:
No worthy is left in the wild; all under heaven are one family. Without fame, without proclaimed virtue — radiance illuminates China.

Vernacular: No worthy person is left overlooked in the countryside; all under heaven become one family. Without personal fame or proclaimed virtue, radiance illuminates the land of China.

prophecies.a:
占得此课，易数乃终。前古后今，其道无穷。

白话文翻译：占到这一课，易数推演到此终结。从远古到未来，天道的运行永无穷尽。
prophecies.a_en:
Having drawn this lesson, the calculations of Yi come to an end. From ancient past to future present, the Way is infinite.

Vernacular: Upon reaching this final lesson, the divination of the I Ching concludes. From the distant past to the endless future, the Way continues without limit.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 此为马前课终篇总论，与推背图第六十象类似，以收束全书。表明易数推演至此结束，天道循环无穷。此课不预言具体事件，而是阐述哲学观点，标志着预言的终结。
prophecies.verdict_en: This is the concluding overview of Ma Qian Ke, similar to Image 60 of Tui Bei Tu, serving as the book's closure. It states that the I Ching divination ends here, while the Way cycles endlessly. This lesson does not predict specific events but expresses a philosophical perspective, marking the end of the prophecies.
